content: We can reframe semantic network alignment for cross-domain knowledge fusion as an intrinsically rewarded exploration task for an autonomous agent, where the ICM curiosity mechanism drives the agent to search for unaligned, semantically novel node pairs between disparate knowledge networks rather than just converging to known alignment mappings. The intrinsic reward from ICM is assigned to semantically ambiguous or under-connected cross-domain node pairs that existing alignment models miss, encouraging the agent to discover unexpected high-value alignment connections.
source_topics: Semantic network alignment for cross-domain knowledge fusion
trigger_topic: Semantic network alignment for cross-domain knowledge fusion
